title: Postal Index Number

* [State of Chhattisgarh](/wiki/Chhattisgarh "Chhattisgarh") * [State of Goa](/wiki/Goa "Goa") * [State of Madhya Pradesh](/wiki/Madhya_Pradesh "Madhya Pradesh") * [State of Maharashtra](/wiki/Maharashtra "Maharashtra")

* [State of Kerala](/wiki/Kerala "Kerala") * [State of Tamil Nadu](/wiki/Tamil_Nadu "Tamil Nadu") * [Union Territory of Puducherry](/wiki/Puducherry_(union_territory) "Puducherry (union territory)") * [Union Territory of Lakshadweep](/wiki/Lakshadweep "Lakshadweep")

East | * [State of Arunachal Pradesh](/wiki/Arunachal_Pradesh "Arunachal Pradesh") * [State of Assam](/wiki/Assam "Assam") * [State of Manipur](/wiki/Manipur "Manipur") * [State of Meghalaya](/wiki/Meghalaya "Meghalaya") * [State of Mizoram](/wiki/Mizoram "Mizoram") * [State of Nagaland](/wiki/Nagaland "Nagaland") * [State of Odisha](/wiki/Odisha "Odisha") * [State of Sikkim](/wiki/Sikkim "Sikkim") * [State of Tripura](/wiki/Tripura "Tripura") * [State of West Bengal](/wiki/West_Bengal "West Bengal") * [Union Territory of Andaman and Nicobar Islands](/wiki/Andaman_and_Nicobar_Islands "Andaman and Nicobar Islands")
APS | * [Army Postal Service](/wiki/Army_Postal_Service "Army Postal Service") (APS) * [Field Post Office](/wiki/Field_post_office "Field post office") (FPO)
JK, LA | [Jammu and Kashmir](/wiki/Jammu_and_Kashmir_(union_territory) "Jammu and Kashmir (union territory)"), [Ladakh](/wiki/Ladakh "Ladakh")
DD | [Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu](/wiki/Dadra_and_Nagar_Haveli_and_Daman_and_Diu "Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu")
AN | [Andaman and Nicobar Islands](/wiki/Andaman_and_Nicobar_Islands "Andaman and Nicobar Islands")

(**PIN**; sometimes [redundantly](/wiki/RAS_syndrome "RAS syndrome") a **PIN Code**)[[note 1]](#cite_note-1) refers to a six-digit code in the [Indian](/wiki/India "India") [postal code](/wiki/Postal_code "Postal code") system used by [India Post](/wiki/India_Post "India Post").

or **Digital Postal Index Number** is India Post's new, precise digital addressing system, using a 10-character alphanumeric code for exact locations (within a 4m x 4m grid) instead of broad pin codes, developed with [IIT Hyderabad](/wiki/IIT_Hyderabad "IIT Hyderabad") and [ISRO](/wiki/ISRO "ISRO") for better logistics, e-commerce, and emergency services by offering a uniform, open-source, and interoperable location standard.
Sometimes incorrectly written as "Pin code", "Pincode", "PINcode", or "pincode".

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Code in the post office numbering or post code system used by India Post

A **Postal Index Number** (**PIN**; sometimes [redundantly](/wiki/RAS_syndrome "RAS syndrome") a **PIN Code**)[[note 1]](#cite_note-1) refers to a six-digit code in the [Indian](/wiki/India "India") [postal code](/wiki/Postal_code "Postal code") system used by [India Post](/wiki/India_Post "India Post").

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Postal_Index_Number&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The PIN system was introduced on 15 August 1972 by [Shriram Bhikaji Velankar](/w/index.php?title=Shriram_Bhikaji_Velankar&action=edit&redlink=1 "Shriram Bhikaji Velankar (page does not exist)"), an additional secretary in the [Government of India's](/wiki/Government_of_India "Government of India") [Ministry of Communications](/wiki/Ministry_of_Communications_(India) "Ministry of Communications (India)").[[1]](#cite_note-India_1974-2)[[2]](#cite_note-Mails_section-3)[[3]](#cite_note-4) The system was introduced to simplify the manual sorting and delivery of mail by eliminating confusion over incorrect addresses, similar place names, and different languages used by the public.[[4]](#cite_note-tamilnadupost.nic.in-5)

## PIN structure

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Postal_Index_Number&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: PIN structure")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/f1/Example_of_Indian_Postal_Index_Number.svg/250px-Example_of_Indian_Postal_Index_Number.svg.png)](/wiki/File:Example_of_Indian_Postal_Index_Number.svg)

An example of a Postal Index Number from [Ujjain](/wiki/Ujjain "Ujjain") in [Madhya Pradesh](/wiki/Madhya_Pradesh "Madhya Pradesh"). **4** indicates the *West postal zone*, **5** indicates *a postal sub-zone in Madhya Pradesh*, **6** indicates the *Ujjain sorting district*, **0** indicates the *Ujjain core area service route*, **01** indicates the *Ujjain Head Office* as the delivery office.

The first digit of a PIN indicates the zone, the second indicates the sub-zone, and the third, combined with the first two, indicates the sorting district within that zone. The final three digits are assigned to individual post offices within the sorting district.

### Postal zones

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Postal_Index_Number&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Postal zones")]

There are nine postal zones in India, including eight regional zones and one functional zone (for the Indian Army). The first digit of a PIN indicates the zone and is allocated over the 9 zones as follows:

| 1st digit of PIN | Zone | States or Union Territories |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | North | * [National Capital Territory of Delhi](/wiki/Delhi "Delhi") * [State of Haryana](/wiki/Haryana "Haryana") * [State of Himachal Pradesh](/wiki/Himachal_Pradesh "Himachal Pradesh") * [State of Punjab](/wiki/Punjab,_India "Punjab, India") * [Union Territory of Chandigarh](/wiki/Chandigarh "Chandigarh") * [Union Territory of Jammu and Kashmir](/wiki/Jammu_and_Kashmir_(union_territory) "Jammu and Kashmir (union territory)") * [Union Territory of Ladakh](/wiki/Ladakh "Ladakh") |
| 2 | * [State of Uttarakhand](/wiki/Uttarakhand "Uttarakhand") * [State of Uttar Pradesh](/wiki/Uttar_Pradesh "Uttar Pradesh") |
| 3 | West | * [State of Gujarat](/wiki/Gujarat "Gujarat") * [State of Rajasthan](/wiki/Rajasthan "Rajasthan") * [Union Territory of Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu](/wiki/Dadra_and_Nagar_Haveli_and_Daman_and_Diu "Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu") |
| 4 | * [State of Chhattisgarh](/wiki/Chhattisgarh "Chhattisgarh") * [State of Goa](/wiki/Goa "Goa") * [State of Madhya Pradesh](/wiki/Madhya_Pradesh "Madhya Pradesh") * [State of Maharashtra](/wiki/Maharashtra "Maharashtra") |
| 5 | South | * [State of Andhra Pradesh](/wiki/Andhra_Pradesh "Andhra Pradesh") * [State of Karnataka](/wiki/Karnataka "Karnataka") * [State of Telangana](/wiki/Telangana "Telangana") |
| 6 | * [State of Kerala](/wiki/Kerala "Kerala") * [State of Tamil Nadu](/wiki/Tamil_Nadu "Tamil Nadu") * [Union Territory of Puducherry](/wiki/Puducherry_(union_territory) "Puducherry (union territory)") * [Union Territory of Lakshadweep](/wiki/Lakshadweep "Lakshadweep") |
| 7 | East | * [State of Arunachal Pradesh](/wiki/Arunachal_Pradesh "Arunachal Pradesh") * [State of Assam](/wiki/Assam "Assam") * [State of Manipur](/wiki/Manipur "Manipur") * [State of Meghalaya](/wiki/Meghalaya "Meghalaya") * [State of Mizoram](/wiki/Mizoram "Mizoram") * [State of Nagaland](/wiki/Nagaland "Nagaland") * [State of Odisha](/wiki/Odisha "Odisha") * [State of Sikkim](/wiki/Sikkim "Sikkim") * [State of Tripura](/wiki/Tripura "Tripura") * [State of West Bengal](/wiki/West_Bengal "West Bengal") * [Union Territory of Andaman and Nicobar Islands](/wiki/Andaman_and_Nicobar_Islands "Andaman and Nicobar Islands") |
| 8 | * [State of Bihar](/wiki/Bihar "Bihar") * [State of Jharkhand](/wiki/Jharkhand "Jharkhand") |
| 9 | APS | * [Army Postal Service](/wiki/Army_Postal_Service "Army Postal Service") (APS) * [Field Post Office](/wiki/Field_post_office "Field post office") (FPO) |

### Sorting district

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Postal_Index_Number&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Sorting district")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/b6/2_digit_postcode_india.png/250px-2_digit_postcode_india.png)](/wiki/File:2_digit_postcode_india.png)

2-digit postcode areas in India (defined through the first two PIN digits)

The third digit of a PIN, combined with the first two digits, represents a specific geographical region (except in the case of the functional zone for the Army) called a sorting district that is headquartered at the main post office of the largest city in the region and is known as the [sorting office](/wiki/Sorting_office "Sorting office"). A state may have one or more sorting districts depending on the volume of mail handled.

| PIN prefix | Postal abbreviation | Region |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 11 | DL | [Delhi](/wiki/Delhi "Delhi") |
| 12–13 | HR | [Haryana](/wiki/Haryana "Haryana") |
| 14–15 | PB | [Punjab](/wiki/Punjab,_India "Punjab, India") |
| 16 | CH | [Chandigarh](/wiki/Chandigarh "Chandigarh") |
| 17 | HP | [Himachal Pradesh](/wiki/Himachal_Pradesh "Himachal Pradesh") |
| 18–19 | JK, LA | [Jammu and Kashmir](/wiki/Jammu_and_Kashmir_(union_territory) "Jammu and Kashmir (union territory)"), [Ladakh](/wiki/Ladakh "Ladakh") |
| 20–28 | UP, UK | [Uttar Pradesh](/wiki/Uttar_Pradesh "Uttar Pradesh"), [Uttarakhand](/wiki/Uttarakhand "Uttarakhand") |
| 30–34 | RJ | [Rajasthan](/wiki/Rajasthan "Rajasthan") |
| 36–39 (except 396) | GJ | [Gujarat](/wiki/Gujarat "Gujarat") |
| 396 | DD | [Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu](/wiki/Dadra_and_Nagar_Haveli_and_Daman_and_Diu "Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu") |
| 40–44 (except 403) | MH | [Maharashtra](/wiki/Maharashtra "Maharashtra") |
| 403 | GA | [Goa](/wiki/Goa "Goa") |
| 45–48 | MP | [Madhya Pradesh](/wiki/Madhya_Pradesh "Madhya Pradesh") |
| 49 | CG | [Chhattisgarh](/wiki/Chhattisgarh "Chhattisgarh") |
| 50 | TG | [Telangana](/wiki/Telangana "Telangana") |
| 51–53 | AP | [Andhra Pradesh](/wiki/Andhra_Pradesh "Andhra Pradesh") |
| 56–59 | KA | [Karnataka](/wiki/Karnataka "Karnataka") |
| 60–66 (except 605) | TN | [Tamil Nadu](/wiki/Tamil_Nadu "Tamil Nadu") |
| 605 | PY | [Puducherry](/wiki/Puducherry_(union_territory) "Puducherry (union territory)") |
| 67–69 (except 682) | KL | [Kerala](/wiki/Kerala "Kerala") |
| 682 | LD | [Lakshadweep](/wiki/Lakshadweep "Lakshadweep") |
| 70–74 (except 737 & 744) | WB | [West Bengal](/wiki/West_Bengal "West Bengal") |
| 737 | SK | [Sikkim](/wiki/Sikkim "Sikkim") |
| 744 | AN | [Andaman and Nicobar Islands](/wiki/Andaman_and_Nicobar_Islands "Andaman and Nicobar Islands") |
| 75–77 | OD | [Odisha](/wiki/Odisha "Odisha") |
| 78 | AS | [Assam](/wiki/Assam "Assam") |
| 790–792 | AR | [Arunachal Pradesh](/wiki/Arunachal_Pradesh "Arunachal Pradesh") |
| 793–794 | ML | [Meghalaya](/wiki/Meghalaya "Meghalaya") |
| 795 | MN | [Manipur](/wiki/Manipur "Manipur") |
| 796 | MZ | [Mizoram](/wiki/Mizoram "Mizoram") |
| 797–798 | NL | [Nagaland](/wiki/Nagaland "Nagaland") |
| 799 | TR | [Tripura](/wiki/Tripura "Tripura") |
| 80–85 | BR, JH | [Bihar](/wiki/Bihar "Bihar"), [Jharkhand](/wiki/Jharkhand "Jharkhand") |
| 90–99 | APS | [Army Postal Service](/wiki/Army_Postal_Service "Army Postal Service") |

### Service route

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Postal_Index_Number&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Service route")]

The fourth digit represents the route on which a delivery office is located in the sorting district.[[4]](#cite_note-tamilnadupost.nic.in-5) This is "0" for offices in the core area of the sorting district.

### Delivery office

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Postal_Index_Number&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Delivery office")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/9b/Somnathpur_pictorial_cancellation.JPG/250px-Somnathpur_pictorial_cancellation.JPG)](/wiki/File:Somnathpur_pictorial_cancellation.JPG)

A post box with its PIN marked on it

The last two digits represent the delivery office within the sorting district starting from "01" which would be the General Post Office (GPO) or head office (HO). The numbering of the delivery office is done chronologically with higher numbers assigned to newer delivery offices. If the volume of mail handled at a delivery office is too large, a new delivery office is created and the next available PIN is assigned.[[5]](#cite_note-6) Thus, two delivery offices situated next to each other will only have the first four digits in common.

### Overseas mail

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Postal_Index_Number&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Overseas mail")]

Special cases where mail needs to be sent to locations overseas is handled in an ad hoc manner. For example, the PIN of [Dakshin Gangotri](/wiki/Dakshin_Gangotri "Dakshin Gangotri") and [Maitri](/wiki/Maitri_(research_station) "Maitri (research station)"), research stations located in [Antarctica](/wiki/Antarctica "Antarctica"), is 403001, which is of the PIN of [Panaji](/wiki/Panaji "Panaji"), [Goa](/wiki/Goa "Goa").[[6]](#cite_note-7)

## Delivery system

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Postal_Index_Number&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Delivery system")]

Each PIN is mapped to exactly one delivery post office which receives all the mail to be delivered to one or lower offices within its jurisdiction, all of which share the same code. The delivery office can either be a General Post Office (GPO), a head office (HO), or a sub-office (SO) which are usually located in urban areas. The post from the delivery office is sorted and routed to other delivery offices for a different PIN or to one of the relevant sub-offices or branch offices for the same PIN. Branch offices (BOs) are located in rural areas and have limited postal services.[[7]](#cite_note-8)

## Digital Postal Index Number

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Postal_Index_Number&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Digital Postal Index Number")]

See also: [National Level Addressing Grid](/wiki/National_Level_Addressing_Grid "National Level Addressing Grid")

**DIGIPIN** or **Digital Postal Index Number** is India Post's new, precise digital addressing system, using a 10-character alphanumeric code for exact locations (within a 4m x 4m grid) instead of broad pin codes, developed with [IIT Hyderabad](/wiki/IIT_Hyderabad "IIT Hyderabad") and [ISRO](/wiki/ISRO "ISRO") for better logistics, e-commerce, and emergency services by offering a uniform, open-source, and interoperable location standard.

## Notes

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Postal_Index_Number&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Notes")]

1. **[^](#cite_ref-1)** Sometimes incorrectly written as "Pin code", "Pincode", "PINcode", or "pincode".
